mission.title: Install and Configure Modelpack on Kubernetes
mission.description: The CNCF ModelPack project provides an open standard for packaging, distributing, and running AI models in cloud-native environments. Installing ModelPack allows you to create standard-compliant implementations that facilitate the deployment of AI/ML project artifacts in a vendor-neutral format.

steps.title: Add the ModelPack Helm repository
steps.description:
First, add the ModelPack Helm repository to your local Helm configuration. This allows you to install ModelPack using Helm.
```bash
helm repo add modelpack https://modelpack.org/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install ModelPack using Helm
steps.description:
Now, install ModelPack in your Kubernetes cluster. This command will create a namespace called 'modelpack' if it doesn't already exist.
```bash
helm install modelpack modelpack/modelpack --namespace modelpack --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the ModelPack pods are running correctly in the 'modelpack' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace modelpack
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the ModelPack deployment to ensure it runs efficiently. Modify the deployment configuration as needed.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment modelpack --namespace modelpack --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access ModelPack
steps.description:
Use port-forwarding to access the ModelPack service locally for testing.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/modelpack --namespace modelpack 8080:80
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
To uninstall ModelPack, first remove the Helm release from your cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall modelpack --namespace modelpack
```

uninstall.title: Clean up resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any remaining custom resources or configurations related to ModelPack.
```bash
kubectl delete all --all --namespace modelpack
kubectl delete namespace modelpack
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your current configurations and data.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace modelpack -o yaml > modelpack-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure you have the latest version of the ModelPack Helm chart.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade ModelPack
upgrade.description:
Perform the upgrade to the latest version of ModelPack.
```bash
helm upgrade modelpack modelpack/modelpack --namespace modelpack
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the ModelPack pods are running the new version correctly.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace modelpack
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If your pods are stuck in a Pending state, check for insufficient resources in your cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace modelpack
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the ModelPack service, ensure that the service is running and check the logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace modelpack
```

troubleshooting.title: CrashLoopBackOff error
troubleshooting.description:
If you see a CrashLoopBackOff error, check the pod logs for the root cause of the failure.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace modelpack
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of ModelPack will have all pods running in the 'modelpack' namespace, and you will be able to access the service via port-forwarding. Resource limits should be configured to optimize performance.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add modelpack https://modelpack.org/charts
helm repo update
kubectl set resources deployment modelpack --namespace modelpack --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or later, along with Helm and kubectl installed.
